risk_title: Exclusion of PSNP Transitory Caseload from Humanitarian Response Planning

While this approach may be justified during the January–June period each year as beneficiaries receive PSNP cash transfer, it becomes difficult for PSNP beneficiaries requiring temporary assistance to cope if they are excluded during lean season, from July to December.

risk_title: Health facility infrastructure rehabilitation in conflict-affected areas stalled

However, according to MOH's report, renovation activities for 129 facilities across five regions are in progress, with 49 currently being renovated, 33 contracts awarded and 47 undergoing rebidding due to insufficient or unqualified bidders.

risk_title: Health service restoration in conflict-affected areas severely lagging

risk_title: Postnatal care services declining in low-performing regions
risk_summary: Postnatal care service coverage in the four lowest-performing regions (Afar, Benishangul Gumuz, Gambella, Somali) has declined from baseline of 64% to current 52%, attributed to insufficient delivery waiting rooms, low community awareness, budget shortages, and high staff turnover in conflict-affected areas.
evidence_quote: The current achievement of 52% for 2024 not only misses the target but also represents a decline from the baseline of 64%. This was primarily attributed to the insufficient number of deliveries waiting rooms, low community awareness regarding service utilization, budget shortages, and high staff turnover, all of which require strengthening to improve service uptake in these low performing regions.

risk_summary: Climate-resilient WASH projects in drought-prone woredas face implementation challenges related to population displacement and limited accessibility during rainy seasons.
evidence_quote: Site accessibility is poor during rainy season. The Aysaeita–Afambo Multi-Village Rural Water Supply Project is currently ongoing. 17.65% physical progress was achieved in the reporting period with a total physical target of 91.88%.

risk_title: Humanitarian Crisis Coinciding with Pandemic Response
risk_summary: Health worker deployment and resource allocation have been significantly strained by humanitarian crises occurring simultaneously with the COVID-19 pandemic, affecting project implementation priorities and health system capacity.
evidence_quote: Furthermore, in many regions, the pandemic coincided with existing or new humanitarian crises, further stretching resources and demanding additional health worker deployments.

risk_title: Shifting Priorities from Evolving COVID-19 Emergency
risk_summary: Multiple project indicators are lagging and end targets will not be met due to reallocation of health system resources and shifting priorities resulting from the evolving COVID-19 emergency, affecting implementation of planned activities.
evidence_quote: five other indicators are lagging due to shifting priorities resulting from the evolving COVID-19 emergency, which has led to a reallocation of health system resources.

risk_title: Vaccine Hesitancy Affecting Immunization Targets
risk_summary: Vaccine hesitancy, particularly among adolescents and in urban areas, is preventing achievement of vaccination targets and requiring targeted communication strategies to address community concerns.
evidence_quote: End target will not be met due to shifting priorities resulting from the evolving COVID-19 emergency, and as well as vaccine hesitancy among adolescents.

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk Remains High
risk_summary: Political and governance risk is rated as High across all assessment periods (approval, last approved rating, and proposed rating), indicating persistent governance fragility affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_title: Staffing Gaps in Conflict-Affected Regions Impeding Implementation
risk_summary: Severe staffing shortages in newly established regions (Southwest Ethiopia at 26% staffing, South Ethiopia with recruitment delays) are hindering project implementation capacity and oversight in these areas.
evidence_quote: SW Ethiopia has only 26% staffing, and South Ethiopia faces delays in recruiting a Procurement specialist. Increased attention to staffing issues in these regions is urged.

risk_title: Low Implementation Progress in Newly Established Regions
risk_summary: Rural WASH performance in newly established regions is significantly below target (Southwest Ethiopia 27%, South Ethiopia 46%, Central Ethiopia 51%), indicating implementation challenges that may be linked to governance and security concerns in these areas.
evidence_quote: While the performance in most of the regions are over 60% the performance in the newly established regions is low (Southwest Ethiopia 27%, South Ethiopia 46% and Central Ethiopia 51%) and needs special attention and closer follow up and support.

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints in Slow-Performing Regions
risk_summary: NWTT and RWSCs in slow-performing regions require revitalization, indicating governance and institutional capacity challenges affecting project delivery in fragile areas.
evidence_quote: The Program is receiving enhanced governance from NWSC and RWSC, but NWTT and RWSCs in slow-performing regions need revitalization.

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - High Rating Sustained
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as High at both approval and current implementation stages, indicating persistent fragility in the operating environment that could affect project implementation of financial sector reforms.
evidence_quote: Risk Category Rating at Approval Approval Package - 17 Dec 2024 Proposed Rating Political and Governance High High

risk_title: Macroeconomic Instability - High Risk to Financial Sector Project
risk_summary: Macroeconomic risks are rated High and sustained, creating an unstable backdrop for implementing financial sector strengthening reforms and recapitalization of state-owned banks.
evidence_quote: Risk Category Rating at Approval Approval Package - 17 Dec 2024 Proposed Rating Macroeconomic High High

risk_title: Sector Strategies and Policies Instability
risk_summary: Sector strategies and policies remain rated as High risk, suggesting potential shifts in government financial sector policy direction that could undermine project objectives around banking regulation and state bank reforms.
evidence_quote: Risk Category Rating at Approval Approval Package - 17 Dec 2024 Proposed Rating Sector Strategies and Policies High High

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk Elevation
risk_summary: Political and Governance risk category has been rated as 'High' at project approval and remains 'Substantial' in the most recent rating (ISR Seq. 07, May 2024), indicating persistent governance fragility affecting project implementation in Ethiopia.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: High; Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 07 - 10 May 2024: Substantial

risk_title: Macroeconomic Instability Impact on Implementation
risk_summary: Macroeconomic risk has been rated as 'Substantial' consistently from approval through the most recent review, indicating ongoing economic instability that could affect project implementation capacity and sustainability.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Macroeconomic - Rating at Approval: Substantial; Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 07 - 10 May 2024: Substantial

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - High Rating Persistent
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as 'High' in the current SORT assessment, indicating ongoing fragility in the operating environment that could affect project implementation and sustainability of government-led service delivery systems.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance, Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 014 - 22 Jan 2024: High

risk_title: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability - Moderate Risk
risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability is rated as 'Moderate,' suggesting potential challenges in government institutions' ability to sustain project activities and service delivery systems after project closure, particularly given the governance fragility context.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability, Last Approved Rating ISR Seq. 014 - 22 Jan 2024: Moderate

risk_title: Handover and Sustainability Risks in Conflict-Affected Regions
risk_summary: Project activities in Tigray and Amhara regions (fish hatcheries construction) may face implementation and handover challenges due to these regions' recent conflict history and governance fragility, affecting the ability to properly transfer assets to regional government bureaus.
evidence_quote: Supporting fish hatcheries construction & equipment supply in Amhara and Tigray regions. The mission has outlined several follow-up actions for the project team, including: completing infrastructure development... and properly handover to the respected regional government bureaus

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - High Rating Sustained
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as High, indicating significant fragility or instability affecting project implementation in Oromia State, Ethiopia.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High

risk_title: Staff Turnover Risk Due to Uncompetitive Remuneration in Fragile Context
risk_summary: Continued staff turnover risk in the Project Implementation Unit (PIU) due to stagnant/uncompetitive salaries, particularly affecting critical FCV-sensitive positions (MRV, financial management, procurement). This undermines institutional capacity in a high-risk governance environment.
evidence_quote: Staff turnover continues to be a risk due to the current uncompetitive/stagnant remuneration for PIU staff. The risk for staff turnover is particularly high for skills in demand such as the MRV, financial management and procurement.

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain at High level, creating persistent constraints on project implementation in Ethiopia. These risks are directly impacting the ability of implementing agencies to execute procurement and disbursement activities.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_title: Macroeconomic Instability Constraining Project Execution
risk_summary: Macroeconomic risks rated as High are affecting project implementation capacity. Combined with political instability, these create a challenging operating environment for the power sector reform project.
evidence_quote: Macroeconomic High High High

risk_title: Environment and Social Risks Impacting Implementation
risk_summary: Environment and Social risks are rated as High and persistent, potentially affecting project implementation timelines and community engagement in the power sector modernization activities.
evidence_quote: Environment and Social High High High

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - High Rating
risk_summary: The project faces a High political and governance risk rating at approval, which persists as a proposed rating. This reflects underlying fragility in the operating environment that could affect project implementation in Ethiopia.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High

risk_title: Implementation in Conflict-Affected Areas
risk_summary: Component 2 explicitly targets 'conflict affected areas' for service delivery improvements, indicating the project operates in active or recently affected conflict zones that pose implementation risks.
evidence_quote: Improve service delivery by innovative interventions in climate stressed & conflict affected areas

risk_title: Macroeconomic Risk - High Rating
risk_summary: The project carries a High macroeconomic risk rating at both approval and proposed stages, which in the Ethiopian context may reflect currency instability, inflation, or economic fragility that could undermine project implementation and sustainability.
evidence_quote: Macroeconomic High High

risk_title: Ongoing conflict affecting project implementation in multiple areas
risk_summary: The project continues implementation in conflict-affected areas, requiring specific monitoring and mitigation measures to be documented in financial reports.
evidence_quote: The PMU should include a separate note in quarterly IFRs detailing the impact of conflict and proposed mitigation measures, given the continued implementation in conflict areas.

risk_title: Corridor development project overlap creating safeguards complications
risk_summary: Government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa overlaps with project interventions, requiring addendum RAPs and creating implementation risks that must be proactively managed.
evidence_quote: Any overlap between the project interventions and the government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa must be notified to the World Bank team immediately, with proactive mitigation measures.

risk_title: Safeguards compliance delays in Dessie FSTP due to unresolved requirements
risk_summary: Construction of Dessie FSTP has been pending since 2023 awaiting completion of safeguards requirements compliance, indicating governance and coordination challenges.
evidence_quote: In Dessie town, construction has been pending since 2023, awaiting the completion of requirements to comply with safeguards requirements.

risk_title: Coordination gaps between government implementation agencies
risk_summary: Coordination between implementation agencies (MoWE and AAWSA) needs strengthening, with staffing vacancies at town and regional levels not yet addressed, affecting project delivery.
evidence_quote: Coordination between implementation agencies needs strengthening. However, the staffing vacancies at the town and regional levels have not been addressed. MoWE should promptly fill the necessary staff positions in consultation with the regions.

risk_title: Weak contract management and governance affecting multiple FSTP implementations
risk_summary: Pattern of contract extensions in all ongoing FSTP contracts and discrepancies between physical and financial progress indicate serious contract management and governance issues requiring immediate intervention.
evidence_quote: a pattern of contract extensions was noted in all the ongoing Fecal Sludge Treatment Plants (FSTPs) contracts. Discrepancies between physical and financial progress also points to construction and contract management issues. These findings pose serious concerns.

risk_title: Community Access Restrictions and Social Tensions from Flood Protection Works
risk_summary: Dredging and embankment construction in the Awash basin has restricted community access to the river, causing local grievances and social concerns. Communities on both banks report being excluded from flood protection efforts, indicating inadequate consultation and potential conflict between project implementation and community needs.
evidence_quote: The communities living by the Awash River expressed concerns about the restricted access to the river caused by the constructed dike. It was noted that such ES requirements were not included in the contractors' contract documents. The communities residing along the right bank of the Awash River are also affected by seasonal flooding and have requested to be considered in flood protection efforts. This may indicate the need for adequate consultations with project affected communities.

risk_title: Weak Community Awareness of Grievance Mechanisms
risk_summary: Communities in project intervention areas are unaware of project grievance mechanisms, limiting their ability to voice concerns or seek redress. This creates a governance gap that could allow grievances to fester and escalate into broader social tensions.
evidence_quote: The communities in project intervention areas visited were not aware of the project grievance mechanism and thus there is a need to create awareness and ensure the functionality of the grievance mechanisms.

risk_title: Land Acquisition and Resettlement Delays Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: Resettlement planning for land acquisition activities (Amibara Embankment and Automatic Weather Stations) has not been completed prior to civil works commencement, creating potential for displacement disputes and social conflict. The mission flagged urgent need for proportionate resettlement plans.
evidence_quote: Assess and conduct proportionate resettlement plans for activities that require land acquisition such as Amibara Embankment and the Automatic Weather Stations in the selected 100 sites, before the commencement of civil works.

risk_title: Inadequate Community Consultations on Flood Protection Priorities
risk_summary: Field visits revealed that communities affected by seasonal flooding on both banks of the Awash River have not been adequately consulted about flood protection priorities. Communities on the right bank requested inclusion in protection efforts but were not considered in initial project design, indicating governance and consultation gaps.
evidence_quote: The communities residing along the right bank of the Awash River are also affected by seasonal flooding and have requested to be considered in flood protection efforts. This may indicate the need for adequate consultations with project affected communities.

risk_title: State-owned incumbent operator resistance to regulatory reform
risk_summary: Ethio Telecom, the state-owned incumbent with significant market power, is actively opposing proposed regulatory reforms including new class licenses and SMP remedies. The operator cancelled meetings three times during the mission, signaling political resistance to market opening measures that are critical project prior actions.
evidence_quote: Although these proposed measures have strong support with ECA and MinT, they are opposed by Ethio Tel, the state-owned incumbent, who cancelled meetings three times during the course of the mission, though Ethio Tel did participate in the workshop.

risk_title: Government capacity constraints and competing priorities affecting implementation
risk_summary: The Government has requested multiple additional activities beyond current project funding, including 14 regional data centers, Phase 2 digital addressing, and nationwide digital literacy programs. This signals potential pressure to restructure or reallocate funds, which could affect project focus and implementation timelines.
evidence_quote: During the mission, the Government reiterated its request for additional activities that cannot be supported with current project funding. If these activities cannot be undertaken under a future program, such as the IDEA regional MPA, then the Government may request a restructuring to reallocate funds between activities, in line with current priorities.

risk_title: Delays in critical procurement and contract payments affecting implementation momentum
risk_summary: Implementation Progress is rated as Moderately Satisfactory due to delays in payments for contracts and procurement, particularly for large Component 2 items. These delays, combined with currency shortages and customs duty issues (over US$16m), create implementation bottlenecks that could affect project delivery.
evidence_quote: Implementation Progress is rated as Moderately Satisfactory (MS). This rating reflects the delays experienced in payments for contracts and delays in procurement, notably for certain 'big ticket' items under component 2.

risk_title: Protracted conflicts affecting program operations in Amhara and Oromia
risk_summary: PSNP operations are being implemented amidst protracted conflicts in Amhara and Oromia regions, which are creating implementation challenges and requiring program adaptations in conflict-affected areas.
evidence_quote: Over the last six months, PSNP operations were implemented amidst multiple challenges including protracted conflicts in Amhara and Oromia regions, persistent shocks, the cumulative effects of frequent droughts, and a challenging macro-economic situation.

risk_title: Security challenges limiting public works implementation in conflict-affected areas
risk_summary: Security challenges in conflict-affected regions are preventing adequate follow-up and monitoring of public works implementation, creating gaps in program delivery and linkages between components.
evidence_quote: The mission found that linkages between public works and livelihoods sub-components are insufficient in some of the regions due to security challenges and limited follow-up of public works implementation in conflict-affected areas.

risk_title: Governance capacity constraints in pastoral and highland regions during rapid scale-up
risk_summary: Low implementation capacity in newly expanded pastoral and highland regions, combined with late release of capacity development budgets and short timelines, creates risks of improper fund utilization and governance challenges during rapid livelihood program expansion.
evidence_quote: The mission noted that the delays in livelihoods preparatory activities are associated with low implementation capacity and the late release of capacity development budget in the newly expanded 349 livelihoods woredas, mainly in pastoral and highland regions.

risk_title: Risk of improper use of increased livelihood funds due to rapid scaling and short timelines
risk_summary: The mission identified potential risk of improper use of livelihoods capacity development budgets and grant transfers at household level, given the sixfold increase in budget (from ETB 7.3 billion to ETB 80.5 billion) and compressed implementation timelines of only 7 months.
evidence_quote: The mission noted the potential risk of improper use of livelihoods capacity development budgets, and grant transfers at household level, given the increase in funds and short timelines for implementation.

risk_title: Exclusion of PSNP transitory caseload from humanitarian coordination
risk_summary: PSNP beneficiaries are being excluded from humanitarian partner support during the July-December period when public works entitlements end, due to lack of coordination between PSNP and humanitarian actors, creating a protection gap.
evidence_quote: The mission highlighted that the program was not able to come to an agreement with humanitarian partners to include PSNP beneficiaries for transitory support between July and December 2024 when public works clients are no longer receiving their entitlements. Exclusion of the PSNP transitory caseload in the Ethiopia Disaster Risk Management (EDRMC's) official communication of the Belg needs assessment results has been a critical gap causing confusion among humanitarian partners.

risk_title: Governance tensions over food procurement decisions and market price volatility
risk_summary: Tensions between World Bank fiduciary requirements and government procurement preferences, combined with local market prices exceeding international prices and past delivery delays by state-owned ETBC, are creating governance friction over food procurement strategy.
evidence_quote: The mission highlighted that the unit price proposed by ETBC was significantly higher than international market prices. Additionally, past delays in delivery by ETBC were noted, where contract extensions had been repeatedly required. As a result, the World Bank returned the proposed procurement plan, requesting that the MoA provide a detailed justification for the use of direct contracting and ensure that the pricing is reasonable and compliant with procurement regulations.

risk_title: Security-driven project suspensions affecting implementation timelines
risk_summary: Project implementation has experienced multiple security-related suspensions causing substantial delays. Lot 1 was suspended from November 2021 to April 2023 (17 months) due to security issues, with actual work execution not resuming until April 2024. Lot 3 was suspended from November 2023 to November 2024 due to security problems. These suspensions have cumulatively resulted in 128 months of time loss since project effectiveness.
evidence_quote: The project was suspended due to security issues from November 2021 until the contractor remobilized in April 2023... Although the contractor resumed works in April 2023, the actual execution did not start until April 2024 due to the re-establishment and re-mobilization of resources.

risk_title: Lot 2 contractor demobilization due to force majeure with uncertain remobilization
risk_summary: Lot 2 contractor has been demobilized since November 2021 (over 3 years) due to force majeure/security events. While security situation has reportedly improved, contractor remobilization remains uncertain and dependent on ongoing negotiations. Contractor facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized during the suspension period.
evidence_quote: The project was suspended in November 2021, affecting the completion of the remaining works... the contractor's facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized by unknown groups... ERA is negotiating with the contractor to remobilize to the site as soon as possible.

risk_title: Persistent security challenges affecting project site operations and staff safety
risk_summary: Despite reported improvements, security remains a persistent concern affecting project implementation. The document notes that security challenges previously impacted implementation and that ongoing engagement with security offices is required to ensure continuous improvement of security situations at project sites.
evidence_quote: Despite security challenges previously, the project implementation has shown momentum with the attention given to the project by the Government... ERA to continue engaging with the relevant security office at the appropriate level and ensure continuous improvement of the security situation in the respective sites

risk_title: Vandalism and infrastructure damage during security-related suspensions
risk_summary: During project suspension periods, project infrastructure and facilities have been damaged by unknown groups. Lot 2 experienced vandalism of contractor facilities and traffic signs during the November 2021 suspension period, indicating vulnerability of project assets to security-related threats.
evidence_quote: the contractor's facilities and traffic signs have been vandalized by unknown groups

risk_title: Weak grievance redress mechanism functionality indicating limited community engagement
risk_summary: The Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) established for Project Affected Persons shows weak functionality with only 6 complaints reported since the last mission, suggesting either limited community awareness, trust issues, or inadequate accessibility. This weak GRM could mask underlying community tensions or grievances related to land acquisition and compensation.
evidence_quote: weak functionality of Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM) as only 6 complaints related to roadside erosion and dust issues were reported

risk_title: Limited stakeholder consultations affecting social cohesion and project acceptance
risk_summary: Stakeholder consultations have been limited across the three project lots, with only 39 participants engaged since the last mission. This limited engagement could undermine community buy-in and increase tensions, particularly given the project's significant land acquisition impact (7,216 households affected).
evidence_quote: limited stakeholders' consultations are conducted across the three lots (39 participants) since the last mission

risk_title: Uncertainty regarding additional land acquisition and potential displacement
risk_summary: The mission was informed that there could be additional land acquisition requirements for the project. The need to prepare RAP addendums prior to additional land acquisition indicates potential for further displacement and compensation disputes, particularly given the already substantial impact on 7,216 households.
evidence_quote: Mission was informed that there could be additional land acquisition in the project. Mission suggested to prepare a brief addendum to the RAP prior to land acquisition and physical and/or economical relocation of PAPs for any additional land requirement.

risk_title: Weak Project Steering Committee Engagement and Governance Guidance
risk_summary: The Project Steering Committee (PSC), chaired by the Minister of Labor and Skills and comprising key stakeholders, has only held one meeting to date. Critical project decisions on Skills for Jobs focus areas and priority subsectors for Sector Skills Bodies lack PSC guidance at a critical implementation stage, indicating potential governance fragility in project oversight.
evidence_quote: To date, only one meeting of the PSC has been held. The mission requests that the PSC meets regularly to guide implementation of the project. Several aspects of the project including focus areas for windows under the Skills for Jobs and priority subsectors for the Sector Skills Bodies (SSBs) need guidance from the PSC at this critical stage of project implementation.

risk_title: Staffing Gaps in Procurement and Contract Management
risk_summary: The project has been unable to successfully recruit a Procurement/Contract Specialist position after the initial recruitment process failed. This staffing gap affects implementation of major procurement activities worth over 18% of total project financing and may delay critical project activities.
evidence_quote: The current process was not able to identify a candidate successfully. which is currently at the negotiations stage. The mission also guided the team to begin planning to launch the next window by March, 2025 with guidance from the PSC... Finalize the recruitment of the Procurement/Contract Specialist by February 24, 2025.

risk_title: Inadequate Grievance Redress Mechanism and Community Representation
risk_summary: Grievance redress mechanisms for both beneficiary communities and project workers remain incomplete. Grievance committees lack representation from beneficiary communities including women, and focal persons have not received confidentiality training, creating risks for unaddressed complaints and potential community tensions.
evidence_quote: Going forward, the project needs to (i) finalize the grievance redress mechanism for both the beneficiary community as well as for the project workers; (ii) ensure that grievance committees (GRC) has the representation from the beneficiary community including women; (iii) train the focal person of SEA/SH GRC on confidentiality.

risk_title: Incomplete Security Management and SEA/SH Safeguarding Arrangements
risk_summary: Security management focal points have not been appointed at the federal level or within Project Implementation Teams (PITs) to address Sexual Exploitation, Abuse, and Harassment (SEA/SH) issues. Code of Conduct signing by project workers remains incomplete, creating vulnerabilities in worker and beneficiary protection.
evidence_quote: Going forward, the project needs to... (vi) appoint either a focal point or a consultant for security management at the federal level to train and guide PITs; and (vii) nominate a focal point in each of the PITs for addressal of SEA/SH issues, if any. The mission also advised the project to ensure that all workers sign the Code of Conduct on dos and don'ts.

risk_title: Security Issues Affecting Contract Implementation
risk_summary: Security concerns are contributing to delays in contract implementation and construction activities across project sites.
evidence_quote: Security issue, rainy season, delay in effecting compensation and delay in site handover to the contractors are also contributed for the delay.

risk_title: Weak Contract Administration and Monitoring Due to Governance Gaps
risk_summary: Poor coordination between regional and central authorities (MoWE) in contract administration has created governance gaps that undermine project oversight and contractor accountability.
evidence_quote: The contract administration arrangement between the regions and MoWE created a gap in properly supervising the Works contracts. Generally, the mission identified that weak contract monitoring and close follow up by all parties involved is a critical issue that requires immediate action and improve the contract administration system of the project.

risk_title: Staffing Vacancies at Regional and Local Levels Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: Unfilled staff positions at town and regional levels are hampering project implementation capacity, particularly in environmental and social safeguards, sanitation engineering, and monitoring roles.
evidence_quote: However, the staffing vacancies at the town and regional levels have not been addressed. It is recommended that MoWE, in consultation with the regions, urgently fills the required staff positions.

risk_title: Delayed Resettlement and Land Acquisition Processes
risk_summary: Involuntary resettlement processes are significantly delayed across multiple project sites, with RAP/ARAP preparation incomplete for numerous towns, creating risks of project delays and community tensions.
evidence_quote: MoWE must complete the preparation of RAP for the Arba Minch FSTP addressing WB's team comments, initiate or complete RAP/ARAP preparation for the construction of FSTPs in Adigrat, Assela, Bishoftu, Dessie, Gondar, Jigjiga, Jimma, Shashemene and the five towns (Hawassa, Bahir Dar, Adama, Dire Dawa & Mekele), and expedite the implementation of ARAPs for the Harar, Assosa, Nekemte and Debrebirhan towns.

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Utility Management and Coordination
risk_summary: Lack of clear role delineation between municipalities and water and sanitation service enterprises (WSSEs) is undermining effective project governance and service delivery coordination.
evidence_quote: There is no clear role delineation between municipality and WSSE

risk_title: Inadequate Grievance Redress Mechanism Functionality
risk_summary: While GRMs have been established, their functionality remains weak across implementing entities, limiting the ability to effectively address community grievances and potential conflicts.
evidence_quote: Strengthen the functionality of the local level GRMs to effectively record, resolve, monitor and regularly report any grievances related to resettlement and any other project activities.

risk_title: Insecurity and displacement affecting refugee program implementation
risk_summary: Refugee beneficiaries in Pugnido, Gambella experienced insecurity and relocation, causing a large number to drop out of the RHISN program. The government had to re-target 3,175 new beneficiaries to replace dropouts.
evidence_quote: During the previous JRIS mission, the RHISN noted a challenge in Pugnido, Gambella, with a large number of refugee beneﬁciaries dropping out of the program due to insecurity and relocation.

risk_title: Delayed recovery grant distribution to IDPs in conflict-affected Tigray
risk_summary: Recovery grant disbursement to IDPs in Mekelle, Tigray was delayed, though the plan was to disburse by December 2024. This reflects implementation challenges in a conflict-affected region.
evidence_quote: While the recovery grant disbursement was delayed in Mekelle, the plan is to disburse them by December 2024.

risk_title: Limited capacity and service delivery challenges in refugee-hosting locations
risk_summary: Refugee-hosting locations, particularly Pugnido, face critical challenges with Commercial Bank of Ethiopia (CBE) branch capacity, including delays in payment and bank account opening, affecting beneficiary access to cash transfers.
evidence_quote: Finally, there have been delays in payment and bank account opening in some cities, particularly refugee-hosting locations where the capacity of the local CBE branches is considerably low (e.g. Pugnido).

risk_title: Governance and coordination gaps in conflict-affected regions
risk_summary: Implementation challenges in Tigray and other conflict-affected regions, including delayed infrastructure rehabilitation planning and cash transfer implementation for IDPs, suggest governance fragility affecting project execution.
evidence_quote: AF2 targets 116,378 IDPs across six centers, including Tigray (Shire), Amhara (Debre Berhan), Oromia (Shambu), Central Ethiopia (Butajira), Welkite, and Southern Ethiopia (Arba Minch), focusing on cash transfers and grants. Infrastructure rehabilitation plans for Mekelle, Debre Berhan, Woldiya, Bishoftu, Sheger City, and Arba Minch have been ﬁnalized, with site selection, environmental and social safeguards, designs, and Bill of Quantities (BOQs) prepared.

risk_title: Tension between government and development actors on refugee inclusion
risk_summary: Discussion on including host communities in AF2 refugee locations remains ongoing with the World Bank, suggesting potential governance tensions over project scope and refugee policy.
evidence_quote: Including hosts to AF 2 Request Banks's No-Objection for Host Inclusion in speciﬁc 2AF location MUI June 14, 2024 Discussion on going

risk_title: Security concerns affecting mini-grid site implementation
risk_summary: Some mini-grid installation sites are located in areas with security concerns, requiring site replacements and potentially delaying implementation timelines.
evidence_quote: A few problems have been noted: (a) some sites are in areas with security concerns and had to be replaced

risk_title: Corridor development project overlap creating implementation risk
risk_summary: Ongoing government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa may overlap with and impact existing ADELE project distribution line rehabilitation works, affecting 3 out of 50+ feeders.
evidence_quote: The PIU indicated the risk that 3 feeders out of >50 feeders in AA works may be affected by the on-going corridor project of AA administration.

risk_title: Large-scale procurement risk for health and education facilities electrification
risk_summary: Component 4 procurement for 1,337 public facilities poses substantial risk due to scale, bidder qualification, timelines, and sovereign/country-specific risks, with no opportunity to rebid packages that cannot be awarded.
evidence_quote: This procurement, being a very large single public facilities off-grid procurement, poses substantial risk owing to scale, bidder qualification, timelines, and sovereign/country specific risks.

risk_title: Land allocation compliance with World Bank environmental and social standards
risk_summary: EEU must conduct consultations at all mini-grid sites to verify that local authority land allocation processes comply with World Bank E&S rules before installation can proceed, creating a critical path constraint.
evidence_quote: EEU needs to urgently resolve the E&S conditions – conduct consultations at all sites to confirm that the process followed by the local authorities in allocating land to EEU complies with the WB E&S rules. This is on a critical path as the process needs to be complete before installation.

risk_title: Delayed Woreda Selection in Conflict and Drought-Affected Areas
risk_summary: Selection of 23 target woredas (10 conflict-affected, 10 drought-affected, 3 refugee-hosting) remains pending finalization, blocking implementation activities in FCV-affected areas and delaying needs assessment completion.
evidence_quote: Identification of project woredas affected by conflict, drought and high levels of refugees needs to be expedited to commence project implementation activities... MoF management's decision and guidance is critical to finalize the selection. This decision is urgent for the needs assessment and for finalizing the Operation's AWPB for FY2024/25.

risk_title: Incomplete Environmental and Social Risk Management in Conflict-Affected Regions
risk_summary: Critical E&S instruments including Security Risk Assessment, Security Management Plan, and Underserved Communities Planning Framework remain unfinalized, preventing implementation in conflict, drought, and refugee-affected areas.
evidence_quote: The Mission team is concerned about the capacity gap in E&S management as well as the delay in finalizing the ESMF, SEA/SH Prevention and Response Action Plan as well as the Underserved Communities Planning Framework... the delay in finalizing the ESMF, SEA/SH Prevention and Response Action Plan as well as the Underserved Communities Planning Framework.

risk_title: Coordination Gaps Between Government and Development Actors in FCV Regions
risk_summary: Lack of clarity and communication regarding implementation arrangements to implementing agencies, including regional and woreda administrations in conflict and drought-affected areas, has caused delays in providing accurate information on operation progress.
evidence_quote: Capacity gaps, in the Project Coordination Unit (PCU), along with a lack of clarity and communication regarding implementation arrangements to implementing agencies, including regional and woreda administrations, have caused delays in providing accurate and up-to-date information on the Operation's progress.

risk_title: Data Gaps on Refugee and Conflict-Affected Populations
risk_summary: Inconsistent reporting of results framework indicators due to lack of information on refugee populations and data from conflict-affected regions (Amhara and Tigray) is hindering measurement of progress in FCV-affected areas.
evidence_quote: inconsistent reporting of results framework indicators, largely due to data quality and availability issues (e.g., lack of information on Amhara and Tigray regions and reporting on refugees), is also hindering the measurement of progress.

risk_title: Capacity Constraints in Implementing Agencies Across FCV-Affected Levels
risk_summary: Capacity gaps in the PCU and implementing agencies at federal, regional, and woreda levels in conflict and drought-affected areas are limiting coordination and implementation of activities targeting vulnerable populations.
evidence_quote: These challenges are linked to capacity limitations at the PCU... The Mission team is concerned about the capacity gap in E&S management... Critical positions in the PCU need to be filled to improve implementation capacity.

risk_title: Delayed Environmental and Social Management System establishment affecting project implementation
risk_summary: Procurement activities have progressed before establishing a functional ESMS, which is a minimum access condition. Works contracts have been awarded in some regions before the ESMS is established, potentially compromising E&S screening and risk management.
evidence_quote: Some program activities have progressed in procurement before establishing a functional ESMS, which is a minimum access condition. In some regions, works contracts for program activities have been awarded before the ESMS is established.

risk_title: Incomplete Grievance Redress Mechanism limiting conflict resolution capacity
risk_summary: The grievance redress mechanism for the program is not fully established at implementing woreda level, limiting the project's ability to address community complaints and potential grievances that could escalate into conflicts.
evidence_quote: The grievance redress mechanism for the program is not fully established. The team urges the client to ensure that implementing woredas have established a functional GRM and that its implementation is captured in periodic reports.

risk_title: Weak fraud and corruption complaint handling systems at implementing entities
risk_summary: Implementing entities lack functional institutional focal points and systems for recording and reporting complaints on fraud and corruption, which could undermine governance and create tensions between government and development actors.
evidence_quote: Implementing entities need to have functional institutional focal points and systems for recording and reporting complaints on fraud and corruption, procurement, and other issues to increase the transparency and ability of the Ethics and Anti-Corruption or Complaint Handling Officers to analyze and systematically address complaints.

risk_title: Currency depreciation creating financial stress on government matching fund commitments
risk_summary: The Ethiopian Birr has depreciated significantly since project appraisal, threatening the government's ability to meet its 30% matching fund commitment. This could create fiscal tensions and governance challenges in sustaining the program.
evidence_quote: Due to the depreciation of the Birr, current matching funds may not cover the 30% Program expenditure as anticipated. The government should review Program costs and allocate additional matching funds as needed to achieve the Program objective.

risk_title: Inadequate E&S specialist staffing limiting risk management capacity in conflict-affected regions
risk_summary: E&S specialists have not been recruited at federal and regional levels, and E&S focal persons are not assigned at woreda level in most regions. This is particularly concerning in conflict-affected areas like Somali, Tigray, and Afar regions where E&S risks are elevated.
evidence_quote: The program has yet to establish a functional Environmental and Social Management System (ESMS), which includes finalizing the ESMSG, recruiting E&S specialists at both federal and regional program coordination offices, and assigning E&S focal persons with relevant training and experience at the program woredas.

risk_title: Conflict-affected areas limiting project supervision and monitoring
risk_summary: Project areas in Gambella and Benishangul Gumuz regions are affected by conflict, constraining the World Bank's ability to conduct direct supervision and requiring widened internal audit scope and strengthened monitoring mechanisms.
evidence_quote: Some project areas are affected by conflict, such as Gambella and Benishangul Gumuz. The project needs to widen the scope of the internal audit and other monitoring mechanisms.

risk_title: Conflict-affected areas becoming inaccessible to World Bank supervision
risk_summary: Regions affected by conflict (Gambella and Benishangul Gumuz) are becoming inaccessible to World Bank supervision, requiring the project to expand internal audit and monitoring coverage in these regions.
evidence_quote: Widen the coverage of Internal Audit and Monitoring for the regions that became inaccessible to the WB supervision

risk_title: Ethio Telecom Partial Privatization Stalled
risk_summary: The planned partial privatization of Ethio Telecom has not progressed as originally planned, creating uncertainty in the digital market reform agenda and potentially affecting the competitive market environment that the project aims to establish.
evidence_quote: The planned partial privatization of Ethio Telecom had not progressed as originally planned, though a stock market listing in October 2024 is expected to result in the sale of up to 10 percent of shares

risk_title: Uneven Regulatory Playing Field Affecting Market Competition
risk_summary: Safaricom Ethiopia's financial position is adversely impacted by lack of a level playing field with the incumbent operator, and the planned award of a 'License B' has been 'parked' due to inability to attract firms willing to pay the high license fee, undermining competitive market development.
evidence_quote: Safaricom Ethiopia, the new market entrant, now has over six million active subscribers and has constructed over 3,000 new cell towers and uses some 10,000 km of fiber. Safaricom covers some 42 percent of the population directly and is on track for meeting its license obligations with regard to Universal Service. However, its financial position is adversely impacted by the lack of a level playing field with regard to the incumbent operator.

risk_title: Mobile Money Market Restrictions Limiting Digital Financial Services
risk_summary: Government services payments currently accept only Ethio Telecom's telebirr, effectively locking out Safaricom, commercial banks and other digital financial service providers, creating market distortions and limiting the growth of competitive digital financial services.
evidence_quote: Currently, for payments for Government services (e.g. to pay for fuel, taxes etc), only EthioTel's telebirr is accepted, which effectively locks out Safaricom, commercial banks and other providers of digital financial services.

risk_title: Government Budget Reallocation Reducing Project Scope
risk_summary: The Government requested cancellation of US$30 million in project funds to reallocate to other portfolio projects, resulting in removal of problematic activities and shift of digital job creation focus from Component 3 to Component 1, indicating potential governance tensions over project priorities.
evidence_quote: Following a request received from the Government on June 26, 2024, for the cancellation of US$30m in funds from the project so they could be reallocated to other projects in the portfolio, a second restructuring was completed in August 2024. Some US$25m was cancelled from component 3 and US$5m from component 2.

risk_title: Forex Reform Creating Implementation Uncertainty
risk_summary: Significant foreign currency weakening of the Ethiopian Birr since July 2024 creates substantial uncertainty for project implementation, requiring thorough analysis of impacts on planned activities, timelines, and cost escalations, with potential implications for procurement and contract management.
evidence_quote: There has been a significant foreign currency change resulting in substantial weakening of the ETB since the end of July 2024. The Project should conduct a thorough analysis of how this change impacts the planned implementation of project activities.

risk_title: High Customs Duties Creating Budget Pressures
risk_summary: High customs duties on imported goods can almost double contract prices, creating significant budget pressures and implementation challenges, with only selective waivers granted to certain agencies, creating unequal treatment and potential governance concerns.
evidence_quote: A particular concern raised during the project was the high level of customs duties payable on imported goods. In some cases, this can almost double the contract price. The mission urged beneficiaries to follow the example of NIDP which has been granted a waiver on customs duties.

risk_title: Conflict-Related Documentation and Asset Loss in Tigray Region
risk_summary: Project implementation in Tigray region has been affected by conflict, with documented failures to provide financial records at woreda level and inability to verify asset existence, condition, and ownership. Special audit indicates potential loss or damage of project resources.
evidence_quote: The Special Financial Audit Report of the Project part implemented by the Tigray Region for the period from July 8, 2020, to October 11, 2023, has been submitted. The auditor has expressed an unqualified (clean) type of audit opinion, but management letter has indicated failure to provide financial documents of Woredas, inability to verify existence, condition, and ownership of assets, and absence of assessment of lost or damaged assets.

risk_title: Ongoing Conflict Impact on Project Resources in Amhara and Oromia Regions
risk_summary: Active conflicts in Amhara and parts of Oromia region are impacting project implementation, with inadequate assessment of damage to project resources, assets, and financial documents. No comprehensive damage assessment or mitigation plan has been submitted.
evidence_quote: There are currently active conflicts in Amhara and some parts of Oromia region which could have impacted or could impact the Project. Therefore, MoF and MoWE should assess the situation and provide update to the DPs regarding the situation in the Regions and its impact on the Project including on project resources, assets, and financial documents to understand the impact and measures required including updating arrangements if needed.

risk_title: Conflict-Affected Areas Preventing Document Collection and Financial Verification
risk_summary: In conflict-affected woredas and zones, particularly in Amhara region, project documents have been destroyed or misplaced, preventing settlement of expenditures and verification of project activities. This creates fiduciary and implementation risks.
evidence_quote: The only outstanding issue that needs the attention of the finance bureau and MoF is settlement of expenditures that have been made in three Woredas where the documents are either destroyed or misplaced during the conflict with Tigray region.

risk_title: Governance Fragility in Newly Established Regions
risk_summary: Newly established regions (Southwest Ethiopia, South Ethiopia, Central Ethiopia, and Sidama) show significantly slower implementation progress compared to other regions, with low staff deployment rates (25-55%) and weak governance structures, indicating governance capacity challenges.
evidence_quote: Implementation progress in the newly established regions (Southwest Ethiopia, South Ethiopia and Central Ethiopia) is slow compared to the program targets and the performance of other regions. Deployment of key staff is the lowest in SWER and Central Ethiopia regions.

risk_title: Tension Between Government and Development Partners on Project Restructuring
risk_summary: Delays in processing project restructuring for a 2-year no-cost extension due to outstanding procurement and safeguards issues have created tension between government implementing agencies and development partners, affecting project momentum.
evidence_quote: The mission reiterated that procurement and safeguards rating of the project was downgraded as moderately unsatisfactory. This has become serious concern and bottleneck to process restructuring of the project for a no cost extension to allow completion of ongoing contracts.

risk_title: Staff Turnover Due to Low Remuneration Affecting Project Implementation Capacity
risk_summary: High project staff turnover is occurring due to inadequate salary scales set by the Ministry of Finance, creating difficulty in recruiting and retaining qualified personnel. This directly impacts procurement processing, technical capacity, and project implementation timelines.
evidence_quote: The mission is informed that there is serious staff turnover due to low remuneration and large variance in remunerations across World Bank financed projects PMUs. The salary scale set by MoF for the contract staff is not adequate to attract highly qualified and experienced project staff and to retain the existing project staff. The mission noted that 3 technical staff from PCU (EDRMC) have left the project due to lower remuneration.

risk_title: Governance Capacity Constraints in Procurement and Contract Management
risk_summary: Inadequate understanding and application of World Bank procurement rules and procedures by government officials, evaluation committees, and technical teams is creating implementation delays and potential fiduciary risks. This reflects broader governance fragility in project administration.
evidence_quote: The mission noted that the officials, evaluating and endorsing committee members, technical team and procurement specialist involved in the project procurement do not have adequate understanding on the governing procurement rules and procedures which has negative impact on proper implementation of the project procurement. The mission is also informed that there are cases where the Bank's procurement rules and procedures are not strictly followed due to Lack of insightful knowledge on the governing rules.

risk_title: Institutional Coordination Challenges Between Government Agencies
risk_summary: Multiple implementing agencies (MoWE, EDRMC, EMI) with varying capacity levels and coordination mechanisms are creating delays in decision-making, approvals, and implementation. Lengthy review and approval processes across different tiers reflect governance fragmentation.
evidence_quote: The mission is also informed that there are different tiers of reviews and approval of the procurement requests, procurement documents, initiate the procurement activity, recruitment of PIU staff, etc. which has significant impact on the efficiency of the procurement processing at each stage.

risk_title: Security restrictions limiting project oversight and implementation
risk_summary: Security constraints prevent field travel and direct monitoring of project implementation across regions, reducing visibility into implementation quality and FCV-related risks on the ground.
evidence_quote: Due to security restrictions, no field travel was possible for this mission, so meetings were held in Bishoftu.

risk_title: Security issues affecting procurement and implementation pace
risk_summary: Security problems across all DRDIP II implementing regions are hampering implementation progress in region-specific ways, contributing to slow procurement and project delays.
evidence_quote: Security issues, inflation, and capacity gaps on some aspects (particularly irrigation) have led to relatively slow procurement... Security problems are prevalent in all DRIDP II implementing regions, hampering implementation progress in ways specific to each region though some of the challenges are shared by all regions.

risk_title: Capacity gaps in conflict-affected woredas affecting environmental and social safeguards
risk_summary: Challenges maintaining E&S focal persons in conflict-affected woredas are undermining environmental and social risk management implementation, a key safeguard for vulnerable populations including refugees.
evidence_quote: delay replacing the E&S expert in Somali region; and challenges maintaining E&S focal persons in conflict-affected woredas

risk_title: Tigray transition risks from UNOPS to government implementation
risk_summary: Handover of project implementation in Tigray from UNOPS to RPCU has caused delays and performance issues, with over 50% of pending activities in Tigray due to slow UNOPS performance and transition delays.
evidence_quote: More than 50 percent of the activities pending implementation are in Tigray due to slow performance by UNOPS and delays caused by the transition back to the RCPU.

risk_title: Refugee inclusion and social cohesion monitoring gaps
risk_summary: Limited data collection on the impact of social cohesion and inclusion activities between refugees and host communities, creating blind spots in understanding tensions and integration outcomes in displacement-affected areas.
evidence_quote: Limited information was presented on the impact of these activities... The World Bank will include data collection on social cohesion and inclusion in the proposed phone surveys.

risk_title: Refugee livelihood participation significantly below targets
risk_summary: Refugee beneficiary participation in livelihood programs is only 6% against a target of 30%, indicating potential exclusion of refugee populations from income-generating opportunities and economic integration.
evidence_quote: The percentage of refugee beneficiaries is just 6, versus an end target of 30 percent. The project will need to ensure that these targets are met in future financial years.

risk_title: Governance and coordination gaps with government sector offices
risk_summary: Some completed sub-projects are not yet functional because budget for operational costs has not been allocated by the government, indicating weak coordination and commitment from government entities to sustain project investments.
evidence_quote: Some completed sub-projects are not yet functional because budget for operational costs is not allocated yet by the government.

risk_title: Internal control gaps in Somali region affecting disbursement
risk_summary: Internal control gaps observed in Somali RPCU have led to suspension of recommended disbursements from FPCU, indicating governance and fiduciary risks in a conflict-affected region.
evidence_quote: Ensure that internal control gaps observed over payment processing at Somali RPCU are addressed. Recommended disbursement from FPCU to these regions are suspended until concrete actions are taken.

risk_title: Grievance redress system functionality challenges in conflict areas
risk_summary: While GRM structures are being strengthened, there are ongoing challenges in maintaining functional grievance redress mechanisms across all project sites, particularly in conflict-affected woredas where tensions may be high.
evidence_quote: Strengthen the functionality of all the established GRM at all levels to effectively record, resolve and report grievances related to the project implementation.

risk_title: Security concerns affecting ITS installation and road safety pilot activities
risk_summary: Security risks are reported as potential challenges for ITS installation on Lots 1 and 2, and for road safety pilot test implementation. The mission team advised coordination with security institutions to mitigate these risks.
evidence_quote: Major concerns related with shortages of cement supply and security are reported as potential challenges...The security concerns on ITS installation of Lot I and Lot II and on road safety related activities require ERA's and MoTL's proactive action in coordination with relevant institutions.

risk_title: Security concerns hindering NTU consultant operations and pilot test implementation
risk_summary: The NTU consultants implementing post-crash care and road safety enforcement pilots have raised security concerns in selected high-risk road corridors, requiring coordination with security institutions to ensure smooth pilot implementation.
evidence_quote: However, the consultants raised some security concerns in the selected corridors and the bank team advised MoTL to worked with the relevant security institutions to ensure smooth operation of pilot implementations.

risk_title: Community disruptions and damage to project infrastructure
risk_summary: Local communities have caused disruptions during project implementation and are damaging fences on opened sections (Lots 1 and 2), requiring preventive measures and due diligence on incident reporting.
evidence_quote: The project faced initial delays due to challenges with acquisition of construction material sources, late design changes, suspension of works by contractor and disruptions by local communities...preventing damages by the communities on fences of the first two Lots that are opened to traffic and reporting on accidents/incidents doing due diligence.

risk_title: Contractor suspension and work disruptions affecting project timeline
risk_summary: The original contractor suspended works in November 2023, causing significant delays. Although a new contractor was assigned in May 2024, the project remains at critical stage with only 68.3% physical progress and a June 30, 2025 closing date.
evidence_quote: Since the last mission in May 2023, significant progress was achieved up to November 2023 when the contractor suspended the works...The project is at critical stage with recoded to date physical progress of 68.3% given that the closing date is June 30, 2025.

risk_title: Governance transition creating delays in compensation and land acquisition
risk_summary: A new Land Expropriation, Compensation Payment, and Resettlement Proclamation has shifted responsibilities to Regional Administrations, causing delays in compensation payments and removal of obstructions. ERA advised to retain responsibility for outstanding compensation payments.
evidence_quote: The new Proclamation for Land Expropriation, Compensation Payment, and Resettlement gives more responsibilities to the Regional Administrations. Though the change has its own merits, the transition will cause delays in the compensation and removal of obstructions.

risk_title: Pending compensation payments and vulnerable group assistance delays
risk_summary: 127 households remain uncompensated, and vulnerable group assistance and relocation assistance payments are delayed for 105 and 58 PAPs respectively across Lots 2, 3, and 4, creating social tensions.
evidence_quote: However, the following gaps were identified (i) delay in the implementation of the Lot1 and Lot 2 RAP audit corrective action plan; (ii) delay in completing compensation payments for remaining 127 households; (iii) delayed vulnerable groups assistance payment and relocation assistance for 105 and 58 PAPs, respectively

risk_title: Outstanding grievances in Lots 3 and 4 requiring resolution
risk_summary: Unresolved grievances remain in Lots 3 and 4 (7 cases under process in Lot 3, 5 cases under process in Lot 4, and 2 court cases), requiring expedited resolution to prevent escalation.
evidence_quote: The World Bank team advised ERA to expedite the resolution of outstanding grievances in Lots 3 and 4 as well as finalize the outstanding activities related to LRP implementation, and RAP audit corrective action plan.

risk_title: Contractor payment delays and cash flow problems affecting work progress
risk_summary: Lot 4 contractor faces significant cash flow problems due to unpaid foreign currency payments (USD 53.58 million from Chinese EXIM Bank and USD 18.09 million from Government), resulting in reduced work rate and partial suspension of asphalt pavement activities.
evidence_quote: It was reported that the Contractor's foreign currency payments starting from IPC No.3 up to IPC No.36 (i.e., for the works executed from January 2020 up to April 2024) of USD 53. 58 million that has to be covered by the Financer (Chinese EXIM Bank) has not yet been paid to the Contractor and USD 18.09 million has not yet been paid by the Government of Ethiopia. As a result, the Contract has faced cash-flow problem reducing the rate of work and partially stopping the activities on site, such as the asphalt pavement.

risk_title: Assigned contractor on AML sanction list creating payment restrictions
risk_summary: The newly assigned contractor (CCCC) is on the AML sanction list, preventing direct World Bank payments and requiring the client to settle payments from designated account, creating cash flow risks that may impact project completion.
evidence_quote: However, CCCC is in AML sanction list and thus the World Bank will not make direct payments to the Contractor. The client is required to settle payments from the designated account (DA) in a timely manner to avoid any cash flow problem which ultimately may impact timely completion of the project.

risk_title: Security Issues Affecting Project Implementation in Multiple Regions
risk_summary: Security challenges in Amhara, Oromia, and Tigray regions are impeding project implementation activities, including monitoring and data collection efforts.
evidence_quote: Security issue in Amhara and Oromia that affects implementation of activities... the Tigray region has reported that there are limitations in collecting procurement data from respective woreda's to update STEP. In addition, limitation of connection facilities and computers to update STEP data are raised.

risk_title: Inadequate Monitoring Due to Security Constraints
risk_summary: Security issues are preventing adequate environmental and social safeguards monitoring across project implementation areas.
evidence_quote: inadequate monitoring due to security issues... external auditors did not travel to most of the distribution areas during the EFY 2015 annual audit due to security concerns.

risk_title: Regional Restructuring Creating Administrative Coordination Gaps
risk_summary: The restructuring of the former Southern Region into multiple new regions has created coordination gaps in procurement activities and fund management that require resolution.
evidence_quote: restructuring of the former Southern Region... the procurement activities under the previous SNNP region are being handled by the newly established regions under the coordination of the regional procurement specialist assigned for the former SNNP. However, there are reported coordination gaps and the Federal PCU in consultation with the Regions need to resolve the gap as soon as possible.

risk_title: Governance and Fiduciary Concerns in Fund Management
risk_summary: Inter-fund and inter-project borrowing practices, unreconciled balances, and equipment misappropriation indicate governance weaknesses in financial management across implementing entities.
evidence_quote: Inter Project Borrowing has been observed at ATI where LFSDP uses money from other resources. Such practice should be stopped... In the former SNNP Region, the return of the office equipment worth ETB 315,690 has not been confirmed yet which was taken unlawfully for non-project purposes.

risk_title: Audit Findings Indicating Potential Misuse of Project Funds
risk_summary: Multiple audit findings including ineligible expenditures and questionable spending suggest governance and fiduciary control weaknesses affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: For the year ended July 7, 2022 - there is one finding with ineligibility implication in the Amhara region, Kallu woreda, amounting to ETB 112,200... For the year ended July 7, 2023, the audit report was submitted on a due date though the audit opinion was qualified, and multiple questionable expenditures amounting to ETB 3,671,515.22 in total are observed.

risk_title: Weak Governance in Procurement Process Oversight
risk_summary: Deviation from agreed procurement procedures, including cancellation of independent procurement audit without Bank review, indicates governance weaknesses in project management.
evidence_quote: The client reported that they have cancelled the process which again is not in accordance with the agreed procedure which is 'Prior Review'. The cancellation is also against the Bank's advice and agreed action in the previous mission to withhold the process and provide clarification.

risk_title: Conflict-related implementation challenges in Tigray region
risk_summary: Post-conflict environment in Tigray is affecting project implementation, requiring adapted modalities and ongoing security considerations for service delivery.
evidence_quote: the modality was changed due to challenges after the conflict

risk_title: Refugee mobility and security issues impacting RHISN implementation
risk_summary: Difficulties in tracing refugees due to mobility and security issues are delaying livelihood grant disbursement and program enrollment in refugee-hosting areas.
evidence_quote: difficulties in tracing certain refugees due to mobility and security issues

risk_title: Displacement pressures affecting urban refugee targeting in Addis Ababa
risk_summary: Lack of accurate refugee data for out-of-camp refugees in Addis Ababa is delaying implementation planning for youth employment and RHISN components under the second additional financing.
evidence_quote: the mission noted lack of accurately recorded refugee data (particularly urban refugees) on 2nd AF cities, delaying the finalization of quota allocation across 2nd AF locations

risk_title: Hazardous items and explosives impeding public works in conflict-affected areas
risk_summary: Removal of hazardous items and explosives is required before public work implementation can proceed in conflict-affected regions, creating implementation delays.
evidence_quote: Removal of Hazardous items & explosives impeding the implementation of public work

risk_title: IDP caseload and targeting challenges affecting emergency response
risk_summary: Implementation of IDP support in Tigray and Somali regions faces challenges in finalizing city selection and determining appropriate support modalities for displaced populations.
evidence_quote: a revision of the implementation plan for 2AF is required, and the selection of cities that will receive support for carrying out small infrastructure projects needs to be finalized

risk_title: Governance fragility affecting institutional coordination and implementation
risk_summary: Limited institutional linkages between implementing ministries and stakeholders, particularly in emerging regions and cities, are weakening project governance and coordination.
evidence_quote: Limited Institutional linkage between implementing ministries and stakeholders, especially at the emerging regions and city level

risk_title: Staff turnover undermining project implementation capacity
risk_summary: High staff turnover in critical positions (procurement, financial management, project management) due to low remuneration is reducing implementation capacity and institutional continuity.
evidence_quote: the project faced significant staff turnover. In the last year, more than 20 critical staff (Procurement, Financial Management, and Project Management) left the project due to low remuneration and salary

risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - Substantial
risk_summary: Political and governance risks have escalated to Substantial level, affecting project implementation and government commitment to the IPP program.
evidence_quote: The Government of Ethiopia is currently reviewing the IPP program to address systemic hurdles to successful financial close and implementation of projects.

risk_title: Government Program Review Causing Implementation Delays
risk_summary: Government review of the IPP program has resulted in suspension of new project bidding processes, directly impacting project pipeline and implementation timelines.
evidence_quote: In April 2020, the GoE issued an RFP for the Humera, and Gad II sites, along with a possible third site in the Afar region that is yet to be determined, with an anticipated capacity of 150 MWac, 100 MWac, and 125MWac, respectively. However, the process was suspended in early 2021 until the financial close for Gad and Dicheto projects is secured.
